name: Qays ibn Ash'ath
title: Looter of the Cloak
shortDescription: The commander of the Kinda tribe in Yazid's army who stole Imam Hussain's (AS) Qatifa (silk cloak) after his martyrdom.
fullStory:
Qays ibn Ash'ath ibn Qays al-Kindi was the son of Ash'ath (the famous hypocrite of Kufa) and the brother of Ja'da (the killer of Imam Hasan AS). Hypocrisy and enmity towards the Ahl al-Bayt were rooted in his family.

He was one of those who wrote an invitation letter to Imam Hussain (AS), but after Ibn Ziyad's arrival, he changed sides and came to Karbala as one of the main commanders of Umar ibn Sa'd's army (commander of the Kinda tribe).

On the day of Ashura, like other criminals, he engaged in looting. After Imam Hussain's (AS) martyrdom, he looted the Imam's 'Qatifa' (a valuable velvet cloak or towel), which is why he was given the derogatory nickname 'Qays of the Qatifa' in Kufa.

After Mukhtar's uprising, he, along with other killers of Karbala, joined Mus'ab ibn al-Zubayr and participated in various battles, but eventually faced the consequences of his deeds.
quoteArabic: وَسَلَبَ قَطِيفَتَهُ قَيْسُ بْنُ الأَشْعَثِ... فَسُمِّيَ قَيْسَ قَطِيفَة...
quote: (The reason for his shameful reputation:) And Hussain's Qatifa was stolen by Qays ibn Ash'ath... hence he was called 'Qays of the Qatifa'.
